navigation_overview:
The top navigation bar appears on every page and contains the site name "MoodleTest" on the left, which links to the home page, followed by links for Home, Dashboard, and My Courses. On the right side, a notifications bell icon shows alerts when clicked, a messaging icon opens the messaging drawer, and the user's initials in a circular icon opens the user menu dropdown. The user menu contains links for Profile, Grades, Calendar, Private Files, Reports, and Preferences, followed by a Log out option that ends the session and returns to the login page. When viewing a course, a horizontal tab bar appears below the course title with tabs for Course, Participants, Grades, Activities, and Competencies. Students do not see the Settings tab. When viewing an activity, breadcrumbs at the top show the full navigation path from the course, with each segment as a clickable link. The Course Index sidebar on the left side of course pages provides a hierarchical tree of all sections and activities, with expand/collapse arrows per section and a close button (X) to hide the sidebar. The currently active item is highlighted.

---

module_summaries.summary:
Line 1: Users can authenticate by entering Username and Password, log in, access as a guest, or view cookie information (lost password link present but disabled).
Line 2: Displays Username and Password fields, Log in button, Access as a guest and Cookies notice buttons, and inline error messages that clear the password on invalid submission.

Line 1: Users can view a personalized dashboard, filter/search and sort upcoming activities in the Timeline, change the time range, and create new calendar events.
Line 2: Displays a greeting, a Timeline block of upcoming activities with time-range, sort and search controls and empty state, and a monthly Calendar view with course filter and New event button.

Line 1: Users can browse their enrolled courses, filter by status, search, sort and change layout, and use each card menu to star or remove a course from view.
Line 2: Displays course cards showing banner images, clickable course names, category names, and a three-dot menu, with controls for status, search, sort and layout (Card/List/Summary).

Line 1: Users can navigate course content, expand or collapse sections (including Collapse all) and open activities/resources; Edit mode is not available to students.
Line 2: Displays the course name and navigation tabs, collapsible section headers with chevrons, and lists of activities/resources with type icons and clickable names.

Line 1: Users can filter and build compound conditions to locate enrolled users, use alphabetical quick filters, and apply or clear filters.
Line 2: Displays a participants table with checkboxes, sortable first/last name links to profiles, roles, groups, last access, and filter controls including Any toggle, attribute selector and Add condition link.

Line 1: Users can view their personal grade report to inspect grades for individual grade items and the aggregated course total.
Line 2: Displays a collapsible grade table with course header and indented graded activities, showing calculated weight, grade (or “–”), range, percentage, feedback, contribution to course total and an aggregation row for course total.

Line 1: Users can view assignment details and submit work via an online text editor and/or file upload, then monitor submission and grading status.
Line 2: Displays opened and due dates, full description, an Add submission button that opens the submission form, and a Submission status section summarizing submission state, grading status, time remaining, last modified and comments.

Line 1: Users can browse all course activities grouped by type, expand or collapse sections, and navigate directly to any activity by clicking its name.
Line 2: Displays grouped activity sections (Assignments expanded by default) with tables showing Name (clickable with parent section), Due date and Submission status, plus collapsible Forums, Resources and other activity-type sections.

Line 1: Users can view a student's profile, send a message, and access profile editing and related links to courses, posts and reports.
Line 2: Displays the student's circular initials icon, full name, Message button, optional profile description, and information cards for user details (email, visibility, timezone, Edit profile), privacy/policies, course links and reports.

Line 1: Users can log out to terminate the current authenticated session and be redirected to the login page.
Line 2: Displays the Log out control and enforces re-authentication to access protected pages after logout.

test_cases.title: Login with valid credentials redirects to Dashboard
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Fill all required fields (Username, Password) with valid credentials
Click "Log in" button
test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to the Dashboard.

test_cases.title: Access as a guest grants unauthenticated browsing
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Click "Access as a guest" button
test_cases.expected_result: User is granted unauthenticated access and can browse site content without signing in.

test_cases.title: Direct URL access while logged out redirects to login
test_cases.preconditions: User is not logged in
steps: In the browser address bar, navigate directly to a protected page such as Dashboard.
Observe whether the app allows access or changes the page.
test_cases.expected_result: The user is redirected to the login page instead of viewing the protected page.

test_cases.title: Login attempt with invalid credentials shows inline error and clears password
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Fill all required fields (Username, Password) with invalid credentials
Click "Log in" button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline error message is displayed; password field is cleared; username remains populated for correction.
test_cases.spec_evidence: clear the password field, and retain the username

test_cases.title: View cookie usage information via Cookies notice
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Click "Cookies notice" button
test_cases.expected_result: Cookie usage information is displayed (for example, in a dialog or banner) explaining cookie usage.

test_cases.title: Open Cookies notice then access as a guest
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Click "Cookies notice" button
Click "Access as a guest" button
test_cases.expected_result: Cookie information is displayed and the user is then granted unauthenticated access to browse the site without signing in.

test_cases.title: Page refresh while logged in keeps user logged in
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in on an authenticated page
steps: Ensure you are on an authenticated page (for example, Dashboard) while logged in.
Refresh the browser (reload the current page).
Verify the page re-renders and you remain on the authenticated page.
test_cases.expected_result: After refresh the user remains authenticated and is not redirected to the login page.

test_cases.title: Already-logged-in user navigating to login URL is redirected to authenticated landing page
test_cases.preconditions: User is already logged in
steps: While authenticated, navigate to the login URL or open the login page.
Observe where the application navigates you next.
test_cases.expected_result: The user is redirected away from the login page to the authenticated landing page (e.g., Dashboard).

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Leave all required fields empty
Click "Log in" button
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors shown for all required fields.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Empty credentials are treated as invalid

test_cases.title: Lost password? link is disabled on the login page
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Click the "Lost password?" link
test_cases.expected_result: The link is disabled and does not navigate to a recovery page.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Lost password? link is disabled on this test site

test_cases.title: Cookies notice button displays cookie usage information
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Click the Cookies notice button
test_cases.expected_result: Cookie usage information is displayed.

test_cases.title: Enable Edit mode reveals reset/add controls and per-block UI
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Click the Edit mode toggle to enable edit mode
test_cases.expected_result: The "Reset page to default" button appears at the top right; the "+ Add a block" button appears below the Dashboard heading; each block displays a move icon and a three-dot menu.

test_cases.title: "+ Add a block" opens the block types listing (Edit mode required)
test_cases.preconditions: Edit mode enabled
steps: Click the "+ Add a block" button
test_cases.expected_result: A page listing all available block types opens.

test_cases.title: Three-dot menu exposes configure, move, and delete actions for a block
test_cases.preconditions: Edit mode enabled and at least one block present
steps: Click a block's three-dot menu
test_cases.expected_result: The three-dot menu lists configure, move, and delete actions for that block.

test_cases.title: Move icon enables repositioning a block
test_cases.preconditions: Edit mode enabled and at least two blocks present
steps: Click and drag a block using its move icon to a different valid position
Release the block at the target position
test_cases.expected_result: The block is repositioned to the new location.

test_cases.title: Disable Edit mode hides reset/add controls and per-block UI
test_cases.preconditions: Edit mode enabled
steps: Click the Edit mode toggle to disable edit mode
test_cases.expected_result: The "Reset page to default" button, the "+ Add a block" button, move icons, and three-dot menus are not visible.

test_cases.title: Timeline displays upcoming activities across enrolled courses
test_cases.workflow: Timeline filters and search (time range dropdown, sort order dropdown, search field)
test_cases.preconditions: User is on Dashboard and Timeline block is visible with upcoming activities in multiple enrolled courses.
steps: Locate the Timeline block on the Dashboard
Inspect the listed Timeline items and note their course labels
test_cases.expected_result: Timeline shows upcoming activities and deadlines from multiple enrolled courses.

test_cases.title: Using time range dropdown updates displayed Timeline items
test_cases.workflow: Timeline filters and search (time range dropdown, sort order dropdown, search field)
test_cases.preconditions: User is on Dashboard and Timeline block contains items across multiple time ranges.
steps: Open the time range dropdown
Select a different time range option
test_cases.expected_result: Timeline items update to display only activities within the selected time range.

test_cases.title: Using sort order dropdown changes the sort order of Timeline items
test_cases.workflow: Timeline filters and search (time range dropdown, sort order dropdown, search field)
test_cases.preconditions: User is on Dashboard and Timeline block shows at least two items with different dates or priorities.
steps: Open the sort order dropdown within the Timeline block
Select an alternate sort order option
test_cases.expected_result: Timeline items reorder according to the selected sort criterion.

test_cases.title: Search field finds activities by name
test_cases.workflow: Timeline filters and search (time range dropdown, sort order dropdown, search field)
test_cases.preconditions: User is on Dashboard and Timeline block contains an activity with a unique name.
steps: Enter the activity name into the Timeline search field
Trigger the search or apply the search filter
test_cases.expected_result: Search results include activities whose name matches the search query.

test_cases.title: Search field finds activities by type
test_cases.workflow: Timeline filters and search (time range dropdown, sort order dropdown, search field)
test_cases.preconditions: User is on Dashboard and Timeline block contains activities of the specified type.
steps: Enter the activity type into the Timeline search field
Trigger the search or apply the search filter
test_cases.expected_result: Search results include activities whose type matches the search query.

test_cases.title: Create personal calendar entry via New event button
test_cases.workflow: Create personal calendar entry (via "New event" button)
test_cases.preconditions: Calendar block is visible on the Dashboard
steps: Click "New event" in the Calendar block
Fill all required fields (event title, date/time, description or other required fields)
Click "Save" or "Create" to submit the event
test_cases.expected_result: Personal calendar entry is created and the event name appears inline on the corresponding date in the monthly view.

test_cases.title: Monthly view shows current month and year heading
test_cases.workflow: Create personal calendar entry (via "New event" button)
test_cases.preconditions: Calendar block is visible on the Dashboard
steps: Observe the Calendar block's monthly view heading
test_cases.expected_result: The Calendar shows the current month and year as a heading in the monthly view.

test_cases.title: Navigate to next month using right arrow
test_cases.workflow: Navigate between months in Calendar (left/right arrows)
test_cases.preconditions: Calendar block is visible on the page showing a monthly view
steps: Click the Calendar right arrow control
test_cases.expected_result: Monthly heading updates to the next month and year and event indicators for the newly visible month update accordingly.

test_cases.title: Navigate to previous month using left arrow
test_cases.workflow: Navigate between months in Calendar (left/right arrows)
test_cases.preconditions: Calendar block is visible on the page showing a monthly view
steps: Click the Calendar left arrow control
test_cases.expected_result: Monthly heading updates to the previous month and year and event indicators for the newly visible month update accordingly.

test_cases.title: Calendar shows current month and year as monthly heading
test_cases.workflow: Navigate between months in Calendar (left/right arrows)
test_cases.preconditions: Calendar block is visible on the page
steps: Observe the Calendar monthly heading
test_cases.expected_result: The Calendar monthly view displays the current month and year as the heading.

test_cases.title: Current date is highlighted in the Calendar monthly view
test_cases.workflow: Navigate between months in Calendar (left/right arrows)
test_cases.preconditions: Calendar block is visible and showing the current month
steps: Observe the Calendar monthly view
test_cases.expected_result: The cell corresponding to the current date is visually highlighted in the calendar.

test_cases.title: Blocks show move icon and three-dot menu in Edit mode
test_cases.preconditions: Edit mode enabled
steps: Identify an existing block on the dashboard
Observe the block header area for the presence of a move icon and a three-dot menu
test_cases.expected_result: Each block displays both a move icon and a three-dot menu while Edit mode is enabled.

test_cases.title: Three-dot menu exposes configure, move, and delete actions
test_cases.preconditions: Edit mode enabled
steps: Click the three-dot menu for a chosen block
Inspect the menu contents
test_cases.expected_result: The menu lists Configure, Move, and Delete actions for the block.

test_cases.title: Move a block using move controls (triggerable from: move icon, three-dot menu 'Move' action)
test_cases.preconditions: Edit mode enabled and at least two blocks present on the dashboard
steps: Use either the block's move icon or open its three-dot menu and select the Move action
Reposition the block to a different location and complete the move action
test_cases.expected_result: The block's position changes to the new location and the dashboard reflects the updated block order.

test_cases.title: Delete a block via three-dot menu
test_cases.preconditions: Edit mode enabled and the target block is present
steps: Click the three-dot menu for the target block
Click the Delete action and confirm the deletion in the confirmation dialog
test_cases.expected_result: The block is removed from the dashboard and no longer appears in the block list.

test_cases.title: Block-level controls unavailable when not in Edit mode
test_cases.preconditions: Edit mode disabled
steps: Locate an existing block on the dashboard
Attempt to find or interact with the move icon or the block's three-dot menu
test_cases.expected_result: The move icon and three-dot menu are not visible or are not actionable when Edit mode is disabled.
test_cases.spec_evidence: In Edit mode, each existing block shows a move icon and a three-dot menu

test_cases.title: Dates with events display their names inline
test_cases.workflow: Navigate between months in Calendar (left/right arrows)
test_cases.preconditions: Calendar block is visible and at least one event exists in the visible month
steps: Observe dates in the monthly view that have events
test_cases.expected_result: Dates that have events display the event names inline on their date cells.

test_cases.title: Open Configure for a block via the three-dot menu
test_cases.preconditions: Edit mode enabled
steps: Click the three-dot menu for a chosen block
Click the Configure action in the menu
test_cases.expected_result: Block configuration panel or dialog opens allowing configuration of that block.

test_cases.title: "+ Add a block" button not present when Edit mode is off
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Ensure Edit mode is disabled
Check for visibility of the "+ Add a block" button below the Dashboard heading
test_cases.expected_result: The "+ Add a block" button is not visible when Edit mode is off.
test_cases.spec_evidence: "+ Add a block" button appears below the Dashboard heading

test_cases.title: Block move icon and three-dot menu not visible when Edit mode is off
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Ensure Edit mode is disabled
Check a block for the presence of a move icon and a three-dot menu
test_cases.expected_result: Blocks do not display a move icon or a three-dot menu when Edit mode is off.
test_cases.spec_evidence: In Edit mode, each existing block shows a move icon and a three-dot menu

test_cases.title: Selecting a time range with no matching items shows empty state
test_cases.workflow: Timeline filters and search (time range dropdown, sort order dropdown, search field)
test_cases.preconditions: User is on Dashboard and no Timeline items exist within the target time range.
steps: Open the time range dropdown in the Timeline block
Select the time range that contains no items
test_cases.expected_result: An empty state is shown indicating no Timeline items match the selected range.
test_cases.spec_evidence: When no items exist within the selected range, an empty state is shown

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.workflow: Create personal calendar entry (via "New event" button)
test_cases.preconditions: Calendar block is visible on the Dashboard
steps: Leave all required fields empty in the New event form
Click "Save" or "Create"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors shown for all required fields.
test_cases.spec_evidence: "New event" button creates personal calendar entries

test_cases.title: Advance across year boundary using right arrow (month and year update)
test_cases.workflow: Navigate between months in Calendar (left/right arrows)
test_cases.preconditions: Calendar block is visible and showing the last month of a year
steps: Click the Calendar right arrow control once
test_cases.expected_result: Monthly heading advances to the first month of the next year and event indicators for that month are shown as applicable.
test_cases.spec_evidence: left/right arrows navigate between months

test_cases.title: Advance multiple months by repeatedly clicking the right arrow
test_cases.workflow: Navigate between months in Calendar (left/right arrows)
test_cases.preconditions: Calendar block is visible on the page showing a monthly view
steps: Click the Calendar right arrow control multiple times to advance several months
test_cases.expected_result: Monthly heading advances one month per click (including year changes when applicable) and dates with events display their names inline for each newly visible month.
test_cases.spec_evidence: left/right arrows navigate between months

test_cases.title: Course card displays banner, clickable name, and category
test_cases.preconditions: My Courses page is open and contains at least one course card
steps: Locate a course card in the courses grid
Inspect the course card to verify it shows a banner image, the course name rendered as a clickable link, and the category name
test_cases.expected_result: The course card displays a banner image, the course name is a clickable link, and the category name is visible

test_cases.title: Clicking the course name navigates to the course main page
test_cases.preconditions: My Courses page is open with at least one course card
steps: Click the course name link on a course card
Verify the course main page is displayed (course title and course-level navigation elements are visible)
test_cases.expected_result: User is taken to the course main page for that course

test_cases.title: Star this course pins the course to the top of the list
test_cases.preconditions: My Courses page is open and contains multiple course cards with at least one course not already at the top
steps: Open the three-dot menu for a non-top course card
Click the "Star this course" action
Observe the course cards ordering in the grid
test_cases.expected_result: The selected course moves to the top of the courses grid (is pinned to the top)

test_cases.title: Remove from view hides the course from the My Courses listing
test_cases.preconditions: My Courses page is open and contains the target course card
steps: Open the three-dot menu for the target course card
Click the "Remove from view" action
Verify the course no longer appears in the course grid
test_cases.expected_result: The course is hidden from the My Courses listing without changing enrollment

test_cases.title: Controls above the course grid present and offer expected options
test_cases.preconditions: My Courses page is open
steps: Verify four controls appear above the course grid: a status dropdown, a search field, a sort dropdown, and a layout dropdown
Open the status dropdown and verify it lists the expected status options; open the layout dropdown and verify it lists the expected layout options
test_cases.expected_result: All four controls are present above the grid and the dropdowns expose the listed options

test_cases.title: Three-dot menu lists course-level actions including Star and Remove
test_cases.preconditions: My Courses page is open and a course card is visible
steps: Open the three-dot menu for a course card
Verify the menu contains the actions for starring the course and removing the course from view
test_cases.expected_result: The three-dot menu displays the actions such as starring the course and removing it from view

test_cases.title: Collapse all collapses every collapsible section
test_cases.preconditions: Course page open with multiple sections expanded
steps: Ensure at least two sections are expanded (use section chevrons to expand if needed)
Click the "Collapse all" link
test_cases.expected_result: All sections collapse simultaneously; section contents are hidden and chevrons indicate collapsed state.

test_cases.title: Collapse all preserves course heading and navigation tab bar
test_cases.preconditions: Course page open with course name and navigation tab bar visible
steps: Click the "Collapse all" link
test_cases.expected_result: Course name remains as the page heading and the navigation tab bar remains displayed directly below the heading.

test_cases.title: Activities and resources show type icon and clickable name within sections
test_cases.preconditions: Course page open with at least one section containing activities or resources
steps: Ensure a section containing activities/resources is expanded
Inspect the listed activities and resources
test_cases.expected_result: Each activity and resource displays a type icon and its name is presented as a clickable link.

test_cases.title: Expand a collapsed section to reveal its activities and resources
test_cases.workflow: Collapse/expand a section using the collapsible chevron
test_cases.preconditions: Course page is open with at least one collapsed section containing activities
steps: Locate a collapsed section in the main content area
Click the section's collapsible chevron to expand it
Verify the activities and resources inside the section are visible
test_cases.expected_result: The section expands and its activities and resources are displayed.

test_cases.title: Collapse an expanded section to hide its activities and resources
test_cases.workflow: Collapse/expand a section using the collapsible chevron
test_cases.preconditions: Course page is open with at least one expanded section containing activities
steps: Locate an expanded section in the main content area
Click the section's collapsible chevron to collapse it
Verify the activities and resources inside the section are hidden
test_cases.expected_result: The section collapses and its activities and resources are hidden.

test_cases.title: Enable and disable Edit mode when user has edit permission
test_cases.preconditions: User is signed in with edit permissions and Course page is open.
steps: Click the "Edit mode" toggle/button to enable edit mode
Verify editing controls or edit UI are available on the Course page
Click the "Edit mode" toggle/button to disable edit mode
Verify editing controls are no longer available
test_cases.expected_result: Edit mode toggles on and off; availability of editing controls matches the toggle state.

test_cases.title: Prevent student from enabling Edit mode
test_cases.preconditions: User is signed in as a student and Course page is open.
steps: Click the "Edit mode" toggle/button on the Course page
test_cases.expected_result: Edit mode is not enabled; the student is prevented from activating edit controls (toggle stays off or a permission message is shown).
test_cases.spec_evidence: Students cannot enable Edit mode on course pages

test_cases.title: Collapse all collapses sections while preserving Course Index sidebar and active item highlight
test_cases.preconditions: Course page open with the Course Index sidebar visible and an active item highlighted
steps: Click the "Collapse all" link
test_cases.expected_result: All sections collapse and the Course Index sidebar remains visible with the previously active item still highlighted.

test_cases.title: Each activity and resource shows a type icon and has a clickable name when section is expanded
test_cases.workflow: Collapse/expand a section using the collapsible chevron
test_cases.preconditions: Course page is open with an expanded section containing activities/resources
steps: Ensure the target section is expanded
For each listed activity/resource in the section, verify a type icon is displayed and the name is clickable
test_cases.expected_result: All activities and resources display a type icon and have clickable names.

test_cases.title: Course name is displayed as page heading with the navigation tab bar immediately following
test_cases.workflow: Collapse/expand a section using the collapsible chevron
test_cases.preconditions: Course page is open
steps: Observe the top of the course page
Verify the course name appears as the page heading and the navigation tab bar is displayed immediately after the heading
test_cases.expected_result: Course name appears as the page heading followed by the navigation tab bar.

test_cases.title: Only the clicked section toggles; other sections remain unchanged
test_cases.workflow: Collapse/expand a section using the collapsible chevron
test_cases.preconditions: Course page is open with at least two sections in different states (one expanded, one collapsed)
steps: Note the current expanded/collapsed state of two adjacent sections
Click the collapsible chevron of one section
Verify only the clicked section's state changes and the other section's state remains as before
test_cases.expected_result: Only the clicked section toggles; other sections are unaffected.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Sections are collapsible

test_cases.title: Clicking Collapse all when sections are already collapsed leaves state unchanged
test_cases.preconditions: Course page open with all sections already collapsed
steps: Click the "Collapse all" link
test_cases.expected_result: No visual change occurs; all sections remain collapsed.
test_cases.spec_evidence: "Collapse all" collapses all sections at once

test_cases.title: Toggling a section twice restores its original expanded/collapsed state
test_cases.workflow: Collapse/expand a section using the collapsible chevron
test_cases.preconditions: Course page is open with a section in a known expanded or collapsed state
steps: Note the current state of a section
Click the section's collapsible chevron to change its state
Click the same chevron again
Verify the section returns to its original state
test_cases.expected_result: After two toggles the section returns to its original state.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Sections are displayed in the main content area and can be collapsed or expanded

test_cases.title: Apply a single filter condition to refine participants list
test_cases.preconditions: Participants page is open and participants are listed in the table.
steps: Select an attribute from the Select attribute dropdown and enter a matching value
Click "Apply filters"

steps: Enable the "Any" toggle

steps: Click the alphabetical button for First name for a chosen initial
test_cases.expected_result: Participants table shows only users whose first name begins with the chosen initial.

test_cases.preconditions: Participants page is open and the participants table is visible.
steps: Click the alphabetical button for Last name for a chosen initial
test_cases.expected_result: Participants table shows only users whose last name begins with the chosen initial.

test_cases.title: Apply filters with a condition value empty shows validation error
test_cases.preconditions: Participants page is open and participants are listed in the table.
steps: Click "+ Add condition"
Select an attribute from the Select attribute dropdown and leave the condition value empty
Click "Apply filters"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error indicating the condition value is required is shown for the incomplete condition row.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Filter system allows building conditions

test_cases.title: Apply filters with no conditions added
test_cases.preconditions: Participants page is open and participants are listed in the table.
steps: Ensure no filter conditions are present
Click "Apply filters"
test_cases.expected_result: Participants table continues to list all enrolled users (no filtering applied).
test_cases.spec_evidence: Participants page lists all users enrolled in the course

test_cases.title: Toggle the Any control updates filter mode state
test_cases.preconditions: Participants page is open and participants are listed in the table.
steps: Toggle the "Any" control to change its state
test_cases.expected_result: The Any toggle reflects the new state and the filter mode UI updates accordingly.

test_cases.title: Clear filters when no conditions present leaves participants list unchanged
test_cases.preconditions: Filter builder is empty (no active conditions)
steps: Ensure no filter conditions are present (no toggle selected and no conditions added)
Click the "Clear filters" button
test_cases.expected_result: Participants table remains showing all enrolled users and the filter builder stays empty.
test_cases.spec_evidence: The Participants page lists all users enrolled in the course

test_cases.title: Select an initial with no matching participants shows empty results
test_cases.workflow: Filter participants by initial using the alphabetical filter buttons for First name and Last name (All and A–Z)
test_cases.preconditions: Participants page is open and at least one alphabetical initial has no matching participants.
steps: Click the alphabetical button for First name for an initial that has no matching participants
test_cases.expected_result: Participants table displays no rows or an empty-state message indicating no matches.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Alphabetical filter buttons for First name and Last name allow quick initial-based filtering

test_cases.title: View own grades via User report
test_cases.workflow: View student's own grades via a User report
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated as a student and the User report (Grades page) is open
steps: Ensure the User report (Grades) is visible on the page
Inspect the Grade table and verify at least one grade item row is listed under the course header
test_cases.expected_result: Grades page displays the student's own grades for the course via a User report.

test_cases.title: Grade column shows earned value for a graded activity
test_cases.workflow: View student's own grades via a User report
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated as a student and the User report (Grades page) is open
steps: Locate a graded activity row in the Grade table
Verify the Grade cell for that activity displays an earned value (numeric or letter) rather than the placeholder
test_cases.expected_result: Earned value is shown in the Grade column for graded items.

test_cases.title: Course header collapsible expands to show indented graded activities
test_cases.workflow: View student's own grades via a User report
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated as a student and the User report (Grades page) is open
steps: Identify the course name shown as a collapsible header in the Grade table
Click the course header to expand it
Verify graded activities are visible and appear indented beneath the course header
test_cases.expected_result: Course name appears as a collapsible header with graded activities indented beneath.

test_cases.title: Grade table displays all expected columns
test_cases.workflow: View student's own grades via a User report
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated as a student and the User report (Grades page) is open
steps: Inspect the Grade table header row
Verify the columns: Grade item, Calculated weight, Grade, Range, Percentage, Feedback, and Contribution to course total are present
test_cases.expected_result: Grade table columns are displayed as specified in the Grade table layout.

test_cases.title: AGGREGATION Course total row shows cumulative course grade
test_cases.workflow: View student's own grades via a User report
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated as a student and the User report (Grades page) is open
steps: Scroll to the bottom of the Grade table
Verify the AGGREGATION Course total row is present and displays a cumulative grade value across weighted items
test_cases.expected_result: AGGREGATION Course total row displays the cumulative grade across all weighted items.

test_cases.title: Prevent student from accessing the full gradebook
test_cases.workflow: View student's own grades via a User report
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated as a student and the User report (Grades page) is open
steps: Click any visible link or control labeled to open the full gradebook from the Grades page
Observe the response (page content, error message, or access restriction)
test_cases.expected_result: Student is prevented from accessing the full gradebook (access denied or full gradebook not displayed).
test_cases.spec_evidence: Students cannot access the full gradebook

test_cases.title: Prevent student from viewing another student's grades
test_cases.workflow: View student's own grades via a User report
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated as a student and the User report (Grades page) is open
steps: Attempt to open a different user's grade view by clicking any student name or user-report link visible on the page
Observe whether grade details for another student are displayed or access is blocked
test_cases.expected_result: Student cannot view other students' grades; other students' grade details are not accessible.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Students cannot access other students' grades

test_cases.title: Ungraded activity displays placeholder "–" in Grade column
test_cases.workflow: View student's own grades via a User report
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated as a student and the User report (Grades page) is open
steps: Locate an activity known to be not yet graded in the Grade table
Verify the Grade cell for that activity displays "–"
test_cases.expected_result: Grade column shows "–" for activities that have not yet been graded.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Grade shows earned value or "–" if not yet graded

test_cases.title: Submit assignment using online text editor
test_cases.preconditions: Assignment page is open and the submission form includes an online text editor.
steps: Click "Add submission"
Fill the online text editor with valid submission content
Click the submission form's submit button
test_cases.expected_result: Submission status changes to 'Submitted for grading'; the Submission status section shows the summary table rows for Submission status, Grading status, Time remaining, Last modified, and Submission comments; Last modified is updated and Grading status shows 'Not graded'.

test_cases.title: Submit assignment using file upload only
test_cases.preconditions: Assignment page is open and the submission form includes a file upload area.
steps: Click "Add submission"
Upload a valid file in the file upload area
Click the submission form's submit button
test_cases.expected_result: Submission status changes to 'Submitted for grading'; the Submission status section shows the summary table rows for Submission status, Grading status, Time remaining, Last modified, and Submission comments; Last modified is updated and Grading status shows 'Not graded'.

test_cases.title: Submit assignment using both online text and file upload
test_cases.preconditions: Assignment page is open and the submission form includes both an online text editor and a file upload area.
steps: Click "Add submission"
Fill the online text editor with valid submission content and upload a valid file
Click the submission form's submit button
test_cases.expected_result: Submission status changes to 'Submitted for grading'; the Submission status section shows the summary table rows for Submission status, Grading status, Time remaining, Last modified, and Submission comments; Last modified is updated and Grading status shows 'Not graded'.

test_cases.title: Edit an existing submission before the due date
test_cases.preconditions: Submission page is open, student has an existing submission, and due date has not passed.
steps: Click the "Edit submission" control on the submission page
Modify the online text editor content and/or attach files in the file upload area as applicable
Click "Save changes" or "Submit"
test_cases.expected_result: Submission updates are saved, Last modified updates, and Submission status shows the submission as updated.

test_cases.title: Edit submission after due date when teacher permits resubmission
test_cases.preconditions: Submission page is open, student has an existing submission, due date has passed, and teacher permits resubmission.
steps: Click the "Edit submission" control
Modify the online text editor content and/or attach files in the file upload area
Click "Save changes" or "Submit"
test_cases.expected_result: Submission updates are saved and Last modified updates reflecting the resubmission.

test_cases.title: View earned grade and written feedback after grading
test_cases.preconditions: Submission page is open and the student's submission has been graded.
steps: Locate the grading area on the submission page
Verify the earned grade is visible and the written feedback is displayed
test_cases.expected_result: Earned grade and written feedback appear on the submission page.

test_cases.title: Prevent editing when due date has passed and teacher does not permit resubmission
test_cases.preconditions: Submission page is open, student has an existing submission, due date has passed, and teacher does not permit resubmission.
steps: Attempt to click the "Edit submission" control or locate edit inputs
Observe availability of edit controls and the submission form
test_cases.expected_result: Editing is not available: edit controls are absent or disabled and the submission cannot be modified.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Student may view or edit their submission if the due date has not passed

test_cases.title: Open submission form via Add submission
test_cases.preconditions: Assignment page is open and the "Add submission" control is visible.
steps: Click "Add submission"
Verify the submission form is displayed (online text editor and/or file upload area present as configured)
test_cases.expected_result: Submission form opens showing the configured online text editor and/or file upload area.

test_cases.title: Assignment page displays opened date, due date, and full description
test_cases.preconditions: Assignment page is open.
steps: Verify the Assignment page displays the Opened date, Due date, and full Description
test_cases.expected_result: Opened date, Due date, and full Description are visible on the Assignment page.

test_cases.title: Submission status summary table displays required rows
test_cases.preconditions: Assignment submission page is open.
steps: Locate the Submission status section on the page
Verify the summary table contains rows for Submission status, Grading status, Time remaining, Last modified, and Submission comments
test_cases.expected_result: Summary table lists Submission status, Grading status, Time remaining, Last modified, and Submission comments.

test_cases.title: Assignment page displays Opened date, Due date, and full Description
test_cases.preconditions: Assignment page is open.
steps: Locate the assignment metadata area on the page
Verify the Opened date, Due date, and the full Description are displayed
test_cases.expected_result: Assignment page shows Opened date, Due date, and full Description.

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.preconditions: Assignment page is open and the submission form is available.
steps: Click "Add submission"
Leave all required submission fields empty
Click the submission form's submit button
test_cases.expected_result: Submission is not accepted and Submission status remains 'No submissions have been made yet'.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Submission status may show 'No submissions have been made yet' or 'Submitted for grading'

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.preconditions: Submission form is open and configured with at least one submission input.
steps: Leave all required submission fields empty
Click "Save changes" or "Submit"
test_cases.expected_result: Submission is not accepted: validation errors are shown or the submission remains in a no-submission state.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Submission status may be 'No submissions have been made yet' or 'Submitted for grading'

test_cases.title: Submission summary shows grading status options after submission
test_cases.preconditions: A valid submission has been made and the Assignment page is open.
steps: Verify the Submission status section's summary table displays the Grading status row
Observe the Grading status value shown in the summary table
test_cases.expected_result: Grading status displays either 'Not graded' or 'Graded' in the summary table.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Grading status may show 'Not graded' or 'Graded'

test_cases.title: Assignments section displays expanded table with Name, Due date, and Submission status
test_cases.preconditions: Course page with activity sections visible.
steps: Observe the Assignments section header on the course page
Verify the section is expanded and displays a table with columns for Name (clickable link with parent section shown below), Due date, and Submission status
test_cases.expected_result: The Assignments section is expanded and the table shows columns for Name (clickable link with parent section shown below), Due date, and Submission status.

test_cases.title: Forums and Resources are collapsed by default and expand via arrow
test_cases.preconditions: Course page with activity sections visible.
steps: Confirm the Forums section and the Resources section are collapsed by default
Click the expand arrow for each collapsed section and verify each section expands to display its activities
test_cases.expected_result: Forums and Resources are collapsed initially and expand to show their activities when the arrow is clicked.

test_cases.title: Clicking an activity name navigates directly to that activity's page
test_cases.preconditions: Course page with at least one activity visible in an expanded section.
steps: Click any activity Name link within an expanded section
Verify the activity's page opens and displays the activity title corresponding to the clicked name
test_cases.expected_result: Clicking any activity name navigates directly to that activity's page.

test_cases.title: Additional activity types appear as distinct collapsible sections and expand
test_cases.preconditions: Course page containing additional activity types.
steps: Locate any additional activity type section on the page
Verify it appears as a collapsible section and click its arrow to expand
Confirm the section expands and its contained activities or resources become visible
test_cases.expected_result: Additional activity types appear as their own collapsible sections and expand to reveal their contents when the arrow is clicked.

test_cases.title: Collapse the Assignments section (expanded by default) hides the assignments table
test_cases.preconditions: Course page with the Assignments section visible and expanded.
steps: Confirm the Assignments section is expanded by default
Click the collapse arrow for the Assignments section
Verify the assignments table is no longer visible and the section displays a collapsed state
test_cases.expected_result: The Assignments table is hidden and the Assignments section shows a collapsed state after clicking the collapse arrow.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Assignments section is expanded by default

test_cases.title: Toggle expand/collapse repeatedly on Forums and Resources to verify stable behavior
test_cases.preconditions: Course page with Forums and Resources sections present.
steps: For each of the Forums and Resources sections, click the expand arrow to expand the section
Click the same arrow to collapse the section, then click again to expand once more
Verify the sections reliably toggle between expanded and collapsed states each time the arrow is clicked
test_cases.expected_result: Forums and Resources reliably toggle between expanded and collapsed states when their arrows are clicked repeatedly.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Forums section and Resources section are expandable via an arrow

test_cases.title: Profile header displays initials, full name, Message button, and optional description
test_cases.preconditions: Profile page is open
steps: Verify the circular initials icon is visible in the header area
Verify the user's full name and the "Message" button are visible
Verify the profile description is displayed when present (optional)
test_cases.expected_result: The header shows the initials icon, full name, Message button, and the profile description when provided.

test_cases.title: User details card shows email, visibility note, timezone, and Edit profile link
test_cases.preconditions: Profile page is open
steps: Inspect the User details information card
Verify the email address, visibility note, timezone, and an "Edit profile" link are present
test_cases.expected_result: User details card lists email address, visibility note, timezone, and an "Edit profile" link.

test_cases.title: Clicking Edit profile opens profile form with collapsible panels and all fields
test_cases.preconditions: Profile page is open
steps: Click "Edit profile" in the User details card

Verify the profile form opens and displays collapsible panels and fields (General, First name, Last name, Email address, Email visibility, MoodleNet profile ID, City/town, Country, Timezone, Description, User picture, Additional names, Interests, Optional fields)
test_cases.expected_result: Profile form opens with collapsible panels and all profile fields available for editing.

test_cases.title: Edit profile: modify fields and save updates are reflected on the profile page
test_cases.preconditions: Profile page of the current student is open
steps: Click "Edit profile"
Modify editable fields (for example First name, Last name, Description, or upload a new user picture)
Click "Save"
test_cases.expected_result: Profile page reflects the updated values after saving.

test_cases.title: Update profile with valid required fields
test_cases.preconditions: Profile form is open.
steps: Fill all required fields (First name, Last name, Email address and other required fields)
Click "Update profile"
test_cases.expected_result: Changes are saved and the profile page reflects the updated values.

test_cases.title: Open profile form via Edit profile
test_cases.preconditions: User is viewing their Profile page.
steps: Click "Edit profile"
test_cases.expected_result: Profile form is displayed and ready for editing.

test_cases.title: Cancel discards edits and returns to profile display
test_cases.preconditions: User is viewing their Profile page.
steps: Click "Edit profile"
Modify one or more editable fields in the profile form
Click "Cancel"
test_cases.expected_result: Profile form closes, changes are not saved, and the profile display shows the original values.

test_cases.title: Edit option not available when viewing another student's profile
test_cases.preconditions: Profile page of another student is open
steps: Inspect the User details card on the other student's profile page
Verify the "Edit profile" link is not present or editing is not available
test_cases.expected_result: Student is unable to access edit functionality; the "Edit profile" link is not visible or editing is blocked.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Students can only modify their own profile

test_cases.title: Prevent student from editing another student's profile
test_cases.preconditions: User is viewing another student's profile page.
steps: Attempt to click "Edit profile" or initiate edit on the displayed profile
test_cases.expected_result: Editing is not permitted for this profile; user cannot make or save changes.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Students can only modify their own profile

test_cases.title: Collapsible panels expand and collapse in Edit profile form
test_cases.preconditions: Profile page is open and profile form is opened
steps: Click "Edit profile" to open the profile form
Expand a collapsible panel and verify its contained fields become visible
Collapse the same panel and verify the contained fields are hidden
test_cases.expected_result: Panels expand to reveal fields and collapse to hide them.

test_cases.title: Open Edit profile form
test_cases.preconditions: User is viewing their own profile page.
steps: Click "Edit profile"
test_cases.expected_result: The profile form opens with editable fields.

test_cases.title: Save profile leaving Description empty
test_cases.preconditions: Profile form is open.
steps: Fill all required fields (First name, Last name, Email address and other required fields), leave Description empty
Click "Update profile"
test_cases.expected_result: Profile is saved successfully and the Description remains empty.

test_cases.title: Upload new user picture and save
test_cases.preconditions: Profile form is open.
steps: In the User picture section select a new picture to upload and optionally fill picture description
Fill all required fields if not already filled
Click "Update profile"
test_cases.expected_result: The user picture is updated to the newly uploaded image on the profile.

test_cases.title: Change email visibility and persist setting
test_cases.preconditions: Profile form is open.
steps: Change the Email visibility setting
Fill all other required fields
Click "Update profile"
test_cases.expected_result: The updated Email visibility setting is saved and reflected on the profile.

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.preconditions: Profile page is open
steps: Click "Edit profile"
Leave all required fields empty
Click "Save"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors shown for all required fields.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Profile description is optional

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.preconditions: Profile form is open.
steps: Leave all required fields empty
Click "Update profile"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors shown for all required fields.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Update profile validates all required fields before saving

test_cases.title: Edit profile action not available when viewing another user's profile
test_cases.preconditions: User is viewing another user's Profile page.
steps: Check the profile page for the presence or availability of the "Edit profile" action
test_cases.expected_result: "Edit profile" action is not present or is disabled for the user.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Students can only modify their own profile

test_cases.title: Save succeeds when Description is left empty (description optional)
test_cases.preconditions: Profile page is open
steps: Click "Edit profile"
Fill other required fields leaving the Description field empty
Click "Save"
test_cases.expected_result: Profile is saved successfully and no description is displayed on the profile page.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Profile description is optional

test_cases.title: Log out ends session and returns to login page
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and top navigation bar is visible
steps: Click the user's initials icon to open the user menu
Click the Log out option in the user menu
test_cases.expected_result: The current authenticated session is terminated and the page redirects to the login page

test_cases.title: Logout from user menu terminates session and shows login page
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in
steps: Open the user menu by clicking the user's initials in the top navigation.
Click the Log out option in the user menu.
Verify the application displays the login page after logging out.
test_cases.expected_result: The session is terminated and the login page is displayed after using the Log out option.

test_cases.title: Protected routes inaccessible after logout
test_cases.preconditions: User has just logged out after an active session
steps: After completing logout, attempt to navigate to a protected page such as Dashboard or Course Page.
Observe whether the protected content loads or access is blocked.
test_cases.expected_result: Access to the protected route is denied and the user is redirected to the login page or shown an access denied state.

test_cases.title: Browser refresh after logout does not restore authenticated session
test_cases.preconditions: User has just logged out
steps: After logging out and seeing the login page, refresh the browser.
Verify whether any authenticated content reappears or the user remains on the login page.
test_cases.expected_result: Refreshing the browser does not restore the authenticated session; the user remains logged out and sees the login page.

test_cases.title: Protected page access requires re-authentication after logout
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and top navigation bar is visible
steps: Click the user's initials icon to open the user menu
Click the Log out option in the user menu
Click the Dashboard link in the top navigation
test_cases.expected_result: Navigation to the protected page is blocked and the user is prompted to log in (redirected to the login page)
test_cases.spec_evidence: Access to all protected pages requires re-authentication after logout

test_cases.title: Browser Back does not restore authenticated session after logout
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and top navigation bar is visible
steps: Click the user's initials icon to open the user menu
Click the Log out option in the user menu
Use the browser Back button
Attempt to click a protected link visible on the page (for example, My Courses or Dashboard)
test_cases.expected_result: User remains logged out; attempts to access protected content require login and redirect to the login page
test_cases.spec_evidence: Access to all protected pages requires re-authentication after logout
